programme.title: Al Jazeera News Hour
programme.desc: In-depth global news from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Doha, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: TRT World News
programme.desc: The latest news from around the globe from TRT World, Istanbul, Turkiye, in English.

programme.title: France 24 Africa News
programme.desc: Join France24's Georja Calvin-Smith for a round up of the week's cultural and current affairs stories from across the African continent. Delivered via satellite from Paris, in English, no subtitles. Watch on SBS VICELAND at 06:30am every Sunday, also available on SBS OnDemand.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: FBC News
programme.desc: Local, world, sport and entertainment news in English from FBC News, Suva Fiji.

programme.title: France 24 Feature
programme.desc: News feature in English from France 24, Paris.

programme.title: euronews
programme.desc: Headlines, Breaking News & Analysis from the Euronews news centre in Brussels, Belgium, in English.

programme.title: Global Us
programme.desc: Deutsche Welle's news magazine looks at the issues that are moving us today and shows how people are living with the opportunities and risks of globalisation.

programme.title: CBC The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news with hosts Adrienne Arsenault and Andrew Chang from Toronto, Canada. Watch on SBS VICELAND at 08:40am everyday, also available on SBS OnDemand.

programme.title: PBS News Horizons
programme.desc: Dive into the world of science, health, technology and environmental issues making headlines each week, hosted by PBS News Hour's William Brangham, with in-depth discussions featuring leading experts and professionals.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton review Bob Roberts, Mac, Howards End, Simple Men, 1492, Malcolm X, Map of the Human Heart, Cousin Bobby. Plus interviews from Directors' Fortnight.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton review My Cousin Vinny, Julia Has Two Lovers, Shadows and Fog, Night Sun. Plus interviews with Joe Pesci, Robert Greenhut and Lily Tomlin.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: David Stratton and Margaret Pomeranz review City of Joy, Let Him Have It, The Hand That Rocks the Cradle, Far and Away. Plus interviews with cast, crew and producers.

programme.title: Searching for Masculinity
programme.desc: This documentary will be the first ever report on the rise of the Manosphere, the key influencers in this space, and their young followers. This online world has monetised the vulnerability of young men searching for role models, and social media is the vehicle to prey on these insecurities.

programme.title: SBS50
programme.desc: SBS Celebrates its 50th anniversary with highlights from the SBS archives.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS Washington Week with the Atlantic
programme.desc: PBS Washington Week with The Atlantic is the longest-running primetime news and public affairs program on television. The show features a group of journalists participating in a weekly roundtable discussion of major news events. Moderated by Jeffrey Goldberg, Editor-in-Chief of The Atlantic.

programme.title: PBS News Compass Points
programme.desc: Join PBS News Hour's Nick Schifrin and a panel of experts each week analysing prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world, breaking down global conflicts, rising tensions, and their implications for U.S. interests.

programme.title: Myths: Greatest Mysteries Of Humanity
programme.desc: El Dorado- the city of gold. Spanish conquistadors searched in vain for its riches. From Peru to Guyana and Colombia, evidence of El Dorado can be found all over South America. But only one of these leads traces back to the origins of the legend.

programme.title: Ange & The Boss: Puskas In Australia
programme.desc: Hungarian football legend Ferenc Puskas arrived in Australia barely speaking English and comedically overweight, yet inspired South Melbourne Hellas to the 1991 NSL Championship. His captain was a young Ange Postecoglou, now Tottenham manager, in this warm, funny, and poignant documentary about sport, identity, and belonging.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A jungle stronghold in Panama, and a mad scientists Italian hilltop haunt.

programme.title: DesiAussie Family Tale of Electric Korma
programme.desc: A look at Electric Korma, a rock band that take Bollywood classics and reinvent them as rock epics. The band talk about their journey, and the importance of culture in music.

programme.title: Women's Amstel Gold Race
programme.desc: All the action from the 2026 Amstel Gold Women's race, as the peloton takes on a 158 km route through the Netherlands, starting in Maastricht and finishing in Berg en Terblijt.

programme.title: Fear Itself With Alex Lee
programme.desc: In this series, comedian Alex Lee (a huge coward) faces her worst nightmares to get to the heart of fear. She'll use VR to overcome her rat phobia, do a hair-raising interview on a rollercoaster, meet a man with a unique phobia of the cosmos and even jump out of a plane. Along the way she'll talk to experts to discover if the culture of fear is real, or if it's all in her head.

programme.title: Amstel Gold Race
programme.desc: All the action from the 2026 Amstel Gold Men's race, as the peloton takes on a 257.4 km route through the Netherlands, starting in Maastricht and finishing in Berg en Terblijt.

programme.title: Conversations with Friends
programme.desc: As the final year at Trinity begins, Bobbi is looking for a room to rent and Frances asks her to move in. Nick returns from Croatia. He and Frances contemplate being more open about their relationship, which excites and frightens her. Stars Alison Oliver, Sasha Lane and Joe Alwyn.

programme.title: Conversations with Friends
programme.desc: At Melissa's book launch, Frances struggles with her growing feelings for Nick. Her jealousy frustrates Bobbi. Feeling alone, Frances seeks intimacy, but the reality of her feelings for Nick cause her to behave in a destructive way. Stars Alison Oliver, Sasha Lane and Joe Alwyn.

programme.title: NHK World English News
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News Hour
programme.desc: In-depth global news from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Doha, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: TRT World News
programme.desc: The latest news from around the globe from TRT World, Istanbul, Turkiye, in English.

programme.title: France 24 Feature
programme.desc: News feature in English from France 24, Paris.

programme.title: The 77 Percent
programme.desc: Africa is home to a large number of youth as they constitute 77 percent of the continent's population. A few ambitious youngsters come together to share their vision for the continent's future.

programme.title: FBC News
programme.desc: Local, world, sport and entertainment news in English from FBC News, Suva Fiji.

programme.title: France 24 Feature
programme.desc: News feature in English from France 24, Paris.

programme.title: euronews
programme.desc: Headlines, Breaking News & Analysis from the Euronews news centre in Brussels, Belgium, in English.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Euromaxx
programme.desc: Magazine programs Euromaxx, Sports Life and other features as broadcast by DW News in Berlin, Germany.

programme.title: PBS News Horizons
programme.desc: Dive into the world of science, health, technology and environmental issues making headlines each week, hosted by PBS News Hour's William Brangham, with in-depth discussions featuring leading experts and professionals.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton report from Sydney and Melbourne Film Festivals with interviews including Armand Assante, Kenneth Anger, John Akomfrah and filmmakers of Black Harvest.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton review Beauty and the Beast, Alien 3, Sleepwalkers, A World Without Pity, Martha and I. Plus interview with Marianne Sagebrecht.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton review Under Suspicion, How to Survive a Broken Heart, Double Take, Batman Returns.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: Pitino: Red Storm Rising
programme.desc: New faces, new goals, same old intensity from Rick Pitino. Coach reflects on a previous rebuild at Kentucky as an early season classic brings back some painful memories.

programme.title: Pitino: Red Storm Rising
programme.desc: As Coach Pitino prepares to face his son, a leader emerges for the Red Storm. In dramatic fashion, Rick returns to a special place that helped launch his career.

programme.title: Inside the Huddle
programme.desc: What is it like to be a footballer? Candid conversations, laughs, and insights that go beyond the game and into the outer.

programme.title: Over the Black Dot
programme.desc: In 2026, Over the Black Dot becomes a digital-first Rugby League yarn free-flowing conversations on footy, culture and community, with panellists and guests sharing authentic Indigenous stories.

programme.title: Stories From The Cities
programme.desc: Stories from the Cities journeys across Canada, Mexico, and the United States, exploring the host cities of the FIFA World Cup 2026. In this episode, we discover the footballing spirit of Toronto through its people, neighbourhoods, culture, and the everyday moments that define the city.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS News Compass Points
programme.desc: Join PBS News Hour's Nick Schifrin and a panel of experts each week analysing prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world, breaking down global conflicts, rising tensions, and their implications for U.S. interests.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News Viceland Afternoon
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: The Curse of Oak Island
programme.desc: After a new Bobby Dazzler is unearthed on Lot 5, the Oak Island team makes a discovery with possible religious implications.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts season fourteen of 8 Out of 10 Cats. Roisin Conaty and Kiri Pritchard-Mclean take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett in the classic words and numbers quiz.

programme.title: Holy Marvels with Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: Ancient legends tell of sacred weapons, mythical swords and monster slaying artifacts. Explore their creation, legendary wielders, and the epic battles that made them immortal.

programme.title: Great Escapes with Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: Quawntay Adams, a young African American man from South-Central Los Angeles, gets caught up in a large-scale marijuana bust and is sentenced to a long prison term. When he finds out he is going to be a father, he determines to break out of his maximum-security cell - which he does.

programme.title: The Hospital: In the Deep End
programme.desc: Former tennis pro Jelena Dokic, Hollywood star Ruby Rose, and food writer Matt Preston return for their final shifts at St Vincent's. Jelena works in oncology to better understand lung cancer, the condition which took the life of her mother-in-law. Ruby works in the Aboriginal Healthcare Unit inspired by her godfather Lionel Rose, and Matt joins staff in neurology to learn more about epilepsy, a condition that claimed his brother nearly 40 years ago.

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Sarah and Rex are abducted by old foe Rupert Mankiewicz, who plans twisted revenge by weaponising Rex. Mark and Jesse race to find them before it's too late.

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: Though the nerds in Big Bang Theory owned the airwaves in the new millennium, sitcoms have always found humour in fish-out-of-water stories, whether about aliens, witches, monsters, nerds, or teens. Bewitched and Sabrina long cast a spell on audiences, while Mork and Mindy give us fresh, funny alien insights into ourselves. In one way or another, audiences relate to outsiders, and these iconic characters give license to laugh at our own foibles in ways few other forms of entertainment do.

programme.title: Dark Side of the Ring
programme.desc: John Tenta couldn't be further from the monstrous 'Earthquake' WWF fans loved to hate. As his career faded, a health battle would threaten to take him from the family he loved.

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: In their final year in Trinity, Marianne and Connell are back together, but whether their relationship will endure after they graduate is uncertain.

programme.title: NHK World English News
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.
